info.title: azul-indexer-dev
info.description:
This is the internal API for Azul's indexer component.


## Contact us

For technical support please file an issue at
[GitHub](https://github.com/DataBiosphere/azul/issues) or email
`azul-group@ucsc.edu`. To report a security concern or misconduct please email
`azul-group@ucsc.edu`.

400.description: Bad request. The request was rejected due to malformed parameters. It must not be retried without modification.

429.description:
Too many requests. 
Clients should wait the number of seconds specified in the
Retry-After response header and then retry the request.

500.description:
Internal server error. 
It is unlikely that a retry will be successful until the problem is
resolved by an operator.

502.description:
Bad gateway. The frontend server received an invalid response from the backend server. 
It is unlikely that a retry will be successful until the problem is
resolved by an operator.

503.description:
Service unavailable. 
Clients should wait the number of seconds specified in the
Retry-After response header and then retry the request.

504.description:
Gateway timeout. The frontend server did not receive a timely response from the backend server. 
It is unlikely that a retry will be successful until the problem is
resolved by an operator.

get.summary: Return OpenAPI specifications for this REST API
get.description:
This endpoint returns the [OpenAPI specifications]'
(https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification) for this REST
API. These are the specifications used to generate the page
you are visiting now.

503.description:
At least one of the checked resources
is not healthy.

The response consists of the following keys:

* `other_lambdas` 
Indicates whether the companion REST API responds to HTTP requests.
* `queues` 
Returns information about the SQS queues used by the indexer and the
mirror.
* `progress` 
The number of Data Store bundles pending to be indexed and the number
of index documents in need of updating.
* `api_endpoints` 
Indicates whether important service API endpoints are operational.
* `elasticsearch` 
Indicates whether the Elasticsearch cluster is responsive.
* `up` 
indicates the overall result of the health check


The top-level `up` key of the response is
`false`.


At least one of the nested `up` keys
is `false`.

504.description:
Gateway timeout. The frontend server did not receive a timely response from the backend server. 
It is unlikely that a retry will be successful until the problem is
resolved by an operator.

200.description:
The checked resources
are healthy.

The response consists of the following keys:

* `other_lambdas` 
Indicates whether the companion REST API responds to HTTP requests.
* `queues` 
Returns information about the SQS queues used by the indexer and the
mirror.
* `progress` 
The number of Data Store bundles pending to be indexed and the number
of index documents in need of updating.
* `api_endpoints` 
Indicates whether important service API endpoints are operational.
* `elasticsearch` 
Indicates whether the Elasticsearch cluster is responsive.
* `up` 
indicates the overall result of the health check


The top-level `up` key of the response is
`true`.


All of the nested `up` keys
are `true`.

get.description:
Health check of the indexer REST API and all
resources it depends on. This may take long time to complete
and exerts considerable load on the API. For that reason it
should not be requested frequently or by automated
monitoring facilities that would be better served by the
[`/health/fast`](#operations-Auxiliary-get_health_fast) or
[`/health/cached`](#operations-Auxiliary-get_health_cached)
endpoints.

get.description:
Health check of only the REST API itself, excluding other
resources that it depends on. A 200 response indicates that
the indexer is reachable via HTTP(S) but nothing
more.

get.summary: Cached health check for continuous monitoring
get.description:
Return a cached copy of the
[`/health/fast`](#operations-Auxiliary-get_health_fast)
response. This endpoint is optimized for continuously
running, distributed health monitors such as Route 53 health
checks. The cache ensures that the indexer is not
overloaded by these types of health monitors. The cache is
updated every minute.

get.description:
Performance-optimized health check of the REST API and other
critical resources tht it depends on. This endpoint can be
requested more frequently than
[`/health`](#operations-Auxiliary-get_health) but
periodically scheduled, automated requests should be made to
[`/health/cached`](#operations-Auxiliary-get_health_cached).

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of keys selecting the health
checks to be performed. Each key corresponds to an
entry in the response.

get.description:
This endpoint allows clients to request a health check on a
specific set of resources. Each resource is identified by a
*key*, the same key under which the resource appears in a
[`/health`](#operations-Auxiliary-get_health) response.

Clients should wait the number of seconds specified in the
Retry-After response header and then retry the request.

200.description: Notification was successfully queued for processing

401.description: Request lacked a valid HMAC header

post.summary: Notify the indexer to perform an action on a bundle
post.description:
Queue a bundle for addition to, or deletion from, the index.
The POST method is ued for additions, the DELETE method for
deletions.

The request must be authenticated using HMAC via the
``signature`` header. Each Azul deployment has its own
unique HMAC key. The HMAC components are the request method,
request path, and the SHA256 digest of the request body.

A valid HMAC header proves that the client is in possession
of the secret HMAC key and that the request wasn't tampered
with while travelling between client and service, even
though the latter is not strictly necessary considering that
TLS is used to encrypt the entire exchange. Internal clients
can obtain the secret key from the environment they are
running in, and that they share with the service. External
clients must have been given the secret key. The now-defunct
DSS was such an external client. The Azul indexer provided
the HMAC secret to DSS when it registered with DSS to be
notified about bundle additions/deletions. These days only
internal clients use this endpoint.

parameters.name: catalog
parameters.description: The name of the catalog to notify.
